headline: Persistent memory for AI agents surged this week as both Microsoft Research and an open protocol effort pushed unified context stores.
body: Microsoft Research introduced Memora, a memory system that separates storage from retrieval and uses harmonic representation to balance abstraction and specificity for long agent tasks, while Open Memory Protocol proposed a single memory store compatible with Claude, ChatGPT, and Curso. On the model side, LongCat-2.0 arrived as a 1.6T-parameter Mixture of Experts model with 48B active, and vibe coding platform Base44 began rolling out its own model as startups chase defensibility through proprietary foundation models. A cluster of technique breakouts, including semantic chunking, masking, ablation, and multi-view consistency, also moved together. Separately, a report that Meta posed as teens to probe rival chatbots about suicide, sex, and drugs raised adversarial robustness and safety concerns.

cited_items.title: Memora: A Harmonic Memory Representation Balancing Abstraction and Specificity
Open Memory Protocol – One Memory Store for Claude, ChatGPT, Curso
LongCat-2.0, a large-scale MoE model with 1.6T total and 48B Active
